title: Multi-select en los chips de filtro rápido de los listados

tasks.title: Define per-facet multi-select filter config model
tasks.description:
Create a single web-side config module (e.g. `apps/web/src/lib/filters/facet-config.ts`) declaring, per filterable facet: `paramKey` (plural array param), `singularParamKey` (existing scalar param kept for back-compat), `operator` (`'AND' | 'OR'`), `enum` (the underlying Zod enum), and `dedicatedLandingPattern` (optional, e.g. `/alojamientos/tipo/{slug}/`).

Represent all four facets from the spec's config table: accommodation type (`types`/`type`, OR, `AccommodationTypeEnum`, dedicated landing), event category (`categories`/`category`, OR, `EventCategoryEnum`, no dedicated landing), blog/post category (`categories`/`category`, OR, `PostCategoryEnum`, dedicated landing), destination attraction (`attractions`, AND, client-side-only, explicitly flagged `outOfBackendScope: true` and NOT touched by any other task — US-12).

Make the shape extensible (a typed array/record) so a sibling issue (HOS-97's gastronomía/experiencias) can add an entry without editing logic elsewhere.

**Files:** new `apps/web/src/lib/filters/facet-config.ts` (or equivalent path matching existing `apps/web/src/lib/filters/` structure).

**Tests (unit, `apps/web`):** AAA — assert each of the 4 facet entries has the exact declared shape/values from the spec table; assert `destinos` entry is `operator: 'AND'` and carries the out-of-backend-scope flag; assert the config is read-only (`as const` / `readonly`) so it can't be mutated at runtime.

**Acceptance mapping:** supports US-1..US-11 (backing config for every multi-select facet); US-12 (destinos documented, not touched).

subtasks.title: Define TS types for facet config entry (paramKey, singularParamKey, operator, enum, dedicatedLandingPattern, outOfBackendScope)
Populate accommodation-type, event-category, blog-category, destination-attraction entries
Write unit tests asserting shape + destinos AND/out-of-scope flag

tasks.title: Add categories array query param to EventSearchHttpSchema
tasks.description:
In `@repo/schemas`, extend `EventSearchHttpSchema` with `categories: createArrayQueryParam('Filter by multiple event categories')` (reuse the existing `createArrayQueryParam` helper from `packages/schemas/src/api/http/base-http.schema.ts`), keeping the existing singular `category` enum field unchanged.

**Files:** `packages/schemas/src/entities/event/event.http.schema.ts` (or wherever `EventSearchHttpSchema` lives — confirm exact path first).

**Tests (unit, `@repo/schemas`):** AAA pattern — (1) schema accepts `categories` as CSV `?categories=A,B` and parses to `['A','B']`; (2) accepts repeated-key form; (3) accepts singular `category` alone with no `categories`; (4) accepts both present simultaneously (schema-level, no precedence logic here — that's the model's job); (5) rejects an invalid enum member in the array with a Zod error; (6) empty string `categories=` parses to `undefined`.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-9 (AC1), US-10 (AC2), US-11 (AC1, AC2).

subtasks.title: Add categories field to EventSearchHttpSchema
Write schema unit tests (CSV, repeated, singular-only, both-present, invalid member, empty)

**Files:** `packages/schemas/src/entities/post/post.http.schema.ts` (confirm exact path).

**Acceptance mapping:** US-9 (AC1), US-10 (AC2), US-11 (AC1, AC2).

tasks.title: Add manual inArray/eq category WHERE branch to EventModel
tasks.description:
In `@repo/db`, add a manual WHERE branch to `EventModel` mirroring `AccommodationModel`'s existing `types`/`type` precedence exactly: `if (params.categories?.length) whereClauses.push(inArray(events.category, params.categories)); else if (params.category) whereClauses.push(eq(events.category, params.category));`. This bypasses the generic `buildWhereClause`, which does NOT convert an array into `inArray` (falls through to invalid `eq(column, arrayValue)`).

**Files:** `packages/db/src/models/event/event.model.ts`.

**Tests (unit, `@repo/db`):** AAA — (1) `categories` with 1 value produces `inArray(events.category, [v])`; (2) `categories` with N values produces `inArray` with all N; (3) only singular `category` present produces `eq`; (4) both present → array wins (assert the built clause reflects `categories`, not `category`); (5) `categories` empty array / undefined → no category clause at all (not `inArray([])`). Assert produced SQL/clause type explicitly (guards the latent `eq(column, arrayValue)` bug from ever regressing).

**Acceptance mapping:** US-2 (AC3, AC5), US-9 (AC2), US-10 (AC3), US-11 (AC2).

subtasks.title: Add inArray/eq precedence branch to EventModel WHERE builder
Write model unit tests (1 value, N values, singular-only, both-present precedence, empty/undefined)

tasks.title: Add manual inArray/eq category WHERE branch to PostModel
tasks.description:
In `@repo/db`, add the same manual WHERE branch to `PostModel` mirroring `AccommodationModel`/`EventModel`: `if (params.categories?.length) whereClauses.push(inArray(posts.category, params.categories)); else if (params.category) whereClauses.push(eq(posts.category, params.category));`.

**Files:** `packages/db/src/models/post/post.model.ts`.

**Tests (unit, `@repo/db`):** same matrix as T-004 applied to `PostModel` — 1 value, N values, singular-only, both-present precedence (array wins), empty/undefined → no clause.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-2 (AC4, AC5), US-9 (AC2), US-10 (AC3), US-11 (AC2).

subtasks.title: Add inArray/eq precedence branch to PostModel WHERE builder
Write model unit tests (1 value, N values, singular-only, both-present precedence, empty/undefined)

tasks.description:

If the service already spreads all HTTP params generically, add an explicit test proving `categories` survives the pass-through rather than assuming it.

**Files:** `packages/service-core/src/services/event/event.service.ts`.

**Tests (integration, `@repo/service-core` + `apps/api`):** (1) service-level test asserting `filters.categories` reaches the model call; (2) API integration test `GET /api/v1/public/events?categories=A,B` returns the union of events with `category ∈ {A,B}`, and `count >= single-value count`; (3) **regression test** — the exact `?category=A,B`-equivalent input (now `?categories=A,B`) that returns nothing/errors on `main` today must match both categories (RED on main, GREEN after this task).

**Acceptance mapping:** US-2 (AC3), US-9 (AC3, AC5), M-2, M-3.

tasks.title: Create buildMultiToggleParamHref helper
tasks.description:
In `apps/web/src/lib/filters/`, add a new helper (sibling to `toggle-query-param.ts`'s `buildToggleParamHref`), e.g. `buildMultiToggleParamHref`, that accumulates/removes a value inside a CSV array query param: adding a value not currently present appends it (or sets the param if absent); clicking an already-present value removes just that value; removing the last remaining value drops the param entirely (no empty `?key=` left behind). It must preserve every other query param (`q`, `sortBy`, other facets) and always drop `page`. RO-RO pattern, named export, JSDoc, Zod-free (pure string/URLSearchParams logic), `import type` where relevant.

**Files:** new `apps/web/src/lib/filters/toggle-multi-query-param.ts` (or colocated in `toggle-query-param.ts` if under 500 lines — match existing file's convention).

**Tests (unit, `apps/web`):** AAA — (1) empty param + add value → `?k=a`; (2) `?k=a` + add `b` → `?k=a,b` (order preserved, append not replace); (3) `?k=a,b,c` + remove `b` → `?k=a,c`; (4) `?k=a` + remove `a` → param removed entirely, not `?k=`; (5) preserves unrelated params (`q`, `sortBy`, other facet keys); (6) always drops `page` regardless of add/remove; (7) adding a third value to `?k=a,b` → `?k=a,b,c` preserving order.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-1 (all AC), US-3 (all AC).

subtasks.title: Implement buildMultiToggleParamHref (add/remove/drop-last, preserve params, drop page)
Write unit tests for add/append/remove/last-removal/preserve/drop-page

tasks.title: Render per-value active state and aria-pressed on chip rows
tasks.description:
Extend the chip-row rendering used by accommodation/event/blog listings (callers of `FilterChips.astro`) so each chip computes `active = values.includes(v)` against the facet's array param values (from `buildMultiToggleParamHref`'s target param), and renders `aria-pressed="true"|"false"` accordingly. `FilterChips.astro` itself stays href-agnostic (per HOS-97) — this task changes the **caller** logic that computes `href`/`active` per chip, not the component's internal markup contract, unless a prop is missing to pass `aria-pressed` through (in which case add it minimally).

**Files:** the accommodation/event/blog listing pages that render `FilterChips` (e.g. under `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/alojamientos/`, `.../eventos/`, `.../publicaciones/`), plus `FilterChips.astro` only if an `aria-pressed` passthrough prop is missing.

**Tests (component, `apps/web`):** AAA — (1) given `?types=HOTEL,CABIN`, the Hotel and Cabaña chips render `aria-pressed="true"`, all others `"false"`; (2) given zero active values, all chips render `aria-pressed="false"`; (3) multiple chips can be simultaneously active (not mutually exclusive).

**Acceptance mapping:** US-5 (AC1), UX Considerations "Multi active visual state".

subtasks.title: Compute active/aria-pressed per chip value from URL param in each listing caller
Add aria-pressed passthrough to FilterChips.astro if missing
Write component tests for multi-active and zero-active states

tasks.title: Add "Clear (N)" bulk-reset chip
tasks.description:
Add a "Limpiar (N)" chip to the chip row that appears only when a facet has 2+ active values, showing the live count `N`. It reuses the existing removable "active filter chip" pattern from `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/alojamientos/index.astro` (~L520-525) for visual/interaction parity. Its href removes the whole facet's array param (via a param-delete, not per-value toggle) while preserving every other param, and drops `page`. Accessible name includes the count, e.g. `aria-label="Limpiar 2 filtros de tipo"` — add the i18n string in `@repo/i18n`.

**Files:** the shared chip-row rendering helper/caller from T-009, plus a new i18n key in `packages/i18n/src/locales/{es,en,pt}/...` (e.g. `filters.clearCount`).

**Tests (component, `apps/web`):** AAA — (1) 2+ active values → "Limpiar (2)" chip renders with correct count; (2) 0 or 1 active value → chip is absent; (3) activating it removes the facet's array param entirely while preserving other params, and drops `page`; (4) `aria-label` includes the numeric count.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-4 (all AC).

subtasks.title: Add Clear(N) chip generation logic reusing removable-chip pattern
Add i18n key for the accessible label with count
Write component tests for appear/absent thresholds, param removal, aria-label

tasks.title: Switch accommodation-type chip to in-place multi-toggle
tasks.description:
On `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/alojamientos/index.astro`, switch the type chip row from dedicated-route navigation (`/alojamientos/tipo/{slug}/`) to in-place `buildMultiToggleParamHref` toggling against the `types` array param (per the facet config from T-001), using the active-state/aria-pressed rendering from T-009 and the Clear(N) chip from T-010. The dedicated landing route itself is NOT modified (stays alive per T-017/US-6) — only the chip's `href` computation changes.

**Files:** `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/alojamientos/index.astro`.

**Tests (component/integration, `apps/web`):** AAA — (1) clicking "Hotel" from an unfiltered listing → URL `?types=HOTEL`; (2) with `?types=HOTEL`, clicking "Cabaña" → `?types=HOTEL,CABIN` (accumulates, not replaces); (3) clicking the active "Hotel" chip while `?types=HOTEL,CABIN` → `?types=CABIN`; (4) clicking a chip while on the dedicated landing that adds a second value navigates to the base listing with both values (`/alojamientos/?types=HOTEL,CABIN`), not the landing.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-1 (AC1, AC2), US-3 (AC1, AC2), US-6 (AC4).

subtasks.title: Replace dedicated-route href with buildMultiToggleParamHref against types param
Verify dedicated landing route untouched
Write accumulate/remove/landing-escape tests

No dedicated landing exists for events categories (per the facet config table), so there is no landing-escape case here.

tasks.title: Rename events sidebar FilterGroup id category to categories
tasks.description:
In the events sidebar configuration for `FilterSidebar.client.tsx`, rename the category `FilterGroup` id from `category` to `categories` so `filter-reducer.ts`'s existing CSV serialization (`params.set(id, values.join(','))`) writes the array param the backend now accepts (T-006). This closes the latent bug where the sidebar already let users tick 2+ categories but serialized under the rejected singular key.

**Files:** the events sidebar filter-group config (wherever `FilterSidebar` is instantiated for the events listing), `filter-reducer.ts` only if it hardcodes the `category` id anywhere (it should not — it's generic).

**Tests (frontend, `apps/web`):** AAA — (1) checking 2 category boxes in the events sidebar and firing navigation writes `?categories=A,B` (not `?category=A,B`); (2) a single checked box still writes a working param (`?categories=A` or backward-compatible `?category=A` per the chosen single-value serialization — assert whichever the reducer actually does); (3) sidebar checkbox state reflects an existing `?categories=A,B` URL on load.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-5 (AC3), US-9 (AC4, AC5), M-3.

tasks.title: Implement shared 2+-value SEO noindex/canonical predicate
tasks.description:
Extend `resolvePromotedFacetCanonical` (`apps/web/src/lib/seo/promoted-facet-canonical.ts`) or add a sibling helper implementing ONE shared predicate, consumed by every affected listing: 0 values → indexable, canonical = base listing (unchanged); exactly 1 value → indexable, canonical = the facet's `dedicatedLandingPattern` from the T-001 config (unchanged existing behavior); 2+ values → `noindex,follow` + canonical = base listing without facet params (new rule, per resolved OQ-1). Must be a single function/predicate, not per-page duplicated logic.

**Files:** `apps/web/src/lib/seo/promoted-facet-canonical.ts` (extend) or a new sibling in the same `apps/web/src/lib/seo/` directory.

**Tests (unit, `apps/web`):** AAA — (1) 0 values → indexable + base canonical; (2) 1 value → indexable + dedicated-landing canonical; (3) 2 values → `noindex,follow` + base canonical (no `?` in the canonical href); (4) 3+ values → same 2+ behavior (not a special 3-value case); (5) predicate is the same function called for accommodations/events/blog (assert via a shared-import test, not copy-pasted logic per page).

**Acceptance mapping:** US-7 (AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5).

subtasks.title: Implement 0/1/2+ predicate extending resolvePromotedFacetCanonical
Write unit tests for the 0/1/2/3+ value matrix
Assert single shared function used, not per-page duplication

tasks.title: Wire 2+-value SEO predicate into accommodations listing head
tasks.description:
Call the shared predicate from T-016 in `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/alojamientos/index.astro`'s `<head>` rendering (robots meta + canonical link), replacing/complementing the existing single-value `resolvePromotedFacetCanonical` call so it now also handles the 2+ case for the `types` param.

**Files:** `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/alojamientos/index.astro`.

**Tests (SEO, `apps/web`):** AAA — (1) `/alojamientos/?types=HOTEL,CABIN` head emits `<meta name="robots" content="noindex,follow">` and canonical = `/alojamientos/` (no query); (2) `/alojamientos/?types=HOTEL` head is indexable with canonical = `/alojamientos/tipo/hotel/`; (3) `/alojamientos/` (0 values) indexable with base canonical; (4) `/alojamientos/tipo/hotel/` (dedicated landing) still resolves 200 and emits its existing canonical, unchanged.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-6 (AC1, AC3), US-7 (AC1-AC4).

subtasks.title: Wire shared predicate into accommodations listing head
Write SEO tests for 0/1/2 value states and dedicated landing

Since events have no dedicated single-value landing, the 1-value case falls back to whatever the predicate defines for a facet without `dedicatedLandingPattern` (indexable, base-listing canonical — confirm this fallback in T-016's implementation).

**Files:** `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/eventos/index.astro`.

**Tests (SEO, `apps/web`):** AAA — (1) `/eventos/?categories=MUSIC,CULTURE` head emits `noindex,follow` + base canonical; (2) `/eventos/?categories=MUSIC` (1 value, no dedicated landing) is indexable with base-listing canonical; (3) `/eventos/` (0 values) indexable with base canonical.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-7 (AC1-AC4).

**Files:** `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/publicaciones/index.astro`.

**Tests (SEO, `apps/web`):** AAA — (1) `/publicaciones/?categories=A,B` head emits `noindex,follow` + base canonical; (2) `/publicaciones/?categories=A` indexable with canonical = `/publicaciones/categoria/{slug}/`; (3) `/publicaciones/` (0 values) indexable, base canonical; (4) `/publicaciones/categoria/{slug}/` dedicated landing still resolves and emits its canonical, unchanged.

tasks.title: Write regression suite for the shipped ?category=A,B latent bug
tasks.description:
Write a dedicated regression integration test suite (`apps/api`) proving the exact input that fails on `main` today — the sidebar already serializes `?category=A,B` for events/blog, which the backend silently rejects/mismatches — now matches both categories once expressed as `?categories=A,B` end-to-end (schema → model → service → route). This suite is explicitly framed as RED before T-006/T-007 land and GREEN after, so it cannot be satisfied by a partial fix.

**Files:** new or extended integration test file(s) under `apps/api/test/` (or the existing events/posts route test files) — e.g. `apps/api/test/routes/public/events.categories-regression.test.ts` and the posts equivalent.

**Tests:** AAA — (1) `GET /api/v1/public/events?categories=MUSIC,CULTURE` returns events of both categories, count > 0 for a fixture with both present; (2) same for `GET /api/v1/public/posts?categories=A,B`; (3) explicitly assert the response is NOT an error and NOT an empty/mismatched set (the exact failure mode being regressed).

**Acceptance mapping:** M-3, US-9 (AC5).

subtasks.title: Write events ?categories=A,B regression test
Write posts ?categories=A,B regression test

tasks.title: Write backward-compatibility regression suite across all three facets
tasks.description:
Write an integration test suite asserting the singular params (`type`, `category`) keep working standalone, and that when both singular and array are present, the array wins — across accommodations, events, and posts. This locks in the precedence rule from the model branches (T-004, T-005, and the pre-existing accommodation model).

**Files:** `apps/api/test/routes/public/` — extend or add test files for accommodations, events, posts covering the backward-compat matrix.

**Tests:** AAA — (1) `GET /accommodations?type=HOTEL` still returns hotels; (2) `GET /events?category=MUSIC` still returns music events; (3) `GET /posts?category=A` still returns category-A posts; (4) `GET /accommodations?type=HOTEL&types=CABIN,APARTMENT` returns cabins+apartments (array wins, not hotel); (5) same both-present-array-wins assertion for events and posts; (6) dedicated landings (`/alojamientos/tipo/hotel/`, `/publicaciones/categoria/{slug}/`) still resolve 200 via their internal singular-value query path.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-10 (all AC).

subtasks.title: Write singular-param-still-works tests for all three facets
Write both-present array-wins tests for all three facets
Verify dedicated landings still resolve via singular path

tasks.title: Write edge-case matrix for array param parsing across layers
tasks.description:
Write a consolidated edge-case test matrix (schema + model + API layers) covering every edge case named in the spec's Testing Strategy that isn't already pinned by T-002/T-003/T-004/T-005's per-task tests: empty array param (`?types=`) → unfiltered, not `IN ()`; duplicate members (`?types=A,A`) → de-duplicated per resolved OQ-4 (`?types=A,A` normalizes to `?types=A`); whitespace/CSV mix (`?types= HOTEL , CABIN `) → `['HOTEL','CABIN']` trimmed; all-values-selected → union equals the fully unfiltered result set; invalid enum member (`?types=HOTEL,NOT_A_TYPE`) → strict `400` per resolved OQ-3. Apply this matrix across all three facets (accommodation `types`, event `categories`, post `categories`) where the case is layer-relevant.

**Files:** extend the schema unit tests (`packages/schemas`) for de-dup + whitespace, and API integration tests (`apps/api`) for empty/all-selected/invalid-400, across the three list endpoints.

**Tests:** AAA per case listed above, one assertion per case per facet where applicable — explicit, not left to interpretation (per the spec's own instruction that de-dup behavior must be asserted, not assumed).

**Acceptance mapping:** US-11 (all AC).

subtasks.title: Write empty-array-param unfiltered tests (3 facets)
Write duplicate-member de-dup tests (3 facets)
Write whitespace-CSV parse tests (3 facets)
Write all-values-selected == unfiltered tests (3 facets)
Write invalid-member strict-400 tests (3 facets)

tasks.title: Write destinos no-op regression guard
tasks.description:
Assert `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/destinos/index.astro`'s filter logic (`activeIds` Set from `?attractions=a,b`, `cardMatchesFilter()` AND, the `is:inline` reconcile + `startViewTransition` script, `showFilters=false`, full-dataset SSR) has NO functional diff after this spec's implementation. Run this after the three chip-switch tasks (T-011/T-012/T-013) land, to catch any accidental shared-helper coupling.

**Files:** no production changes expected — this task is test-only; extend/verify `apps/web` destinos test file(s) if a diff-detection assertion is worth adding (e.g. a snapshot of the filter function's behavior).

**Tests:** (1) run the existing destinos test suite and confirm 100% green with zero modification; (2) add (if not already covered) an explicit assertion that destinos' `AND` semantics and client-side-only behavior are unchanged by the new multi-select facets' code paths (no accidental import of `buildMultiToggleParamHref` or the new facet config into destinos' logic).

**Acceptance mapping:** US-12 (all AC), M-8.

subtasks.title: Run existing destinos suite, confirm green with no modification
Add explicit no-coupling assertion (destinos doesn't import new multi-select helpers)

tasks.title: Write chip/sidebar/URL sync matrix across all three facets
tasks.description:
Write frontend tests proving the chip row and the sidebar always agree, derived solely from the URL query param (no in-memory shared store), across accommodations (`types`), events (`categories`), and blog (`categories`).

**Files:** `apps/web` component/integration test files for the three listing pages and the `FilterSidebar` island.

**Tests:** AAA — (1) given `?types=HOTEL,CABIN`, both the Hotel/Cabaña chips render `aria-pressed="true"` AND the matching sidebar checkboxes render checked; (2) checking a third sidebar box and firing navigation results in the URL gaining that value AND the corresponding chip becoming active on re-render; (3) the events/blog sidebar writes multiple selected categories under the `categories` key, not `category`; (4) navigating directly to a crafted 3-value URL (no prior interaction) alone reproduces the exact selected state in both chip row and sidebar, proving the URL is the single source of truth (no in-memory store).

**Acceptance mapping:** US-5 (all AC).

subtasks.title: Write chip+sidebar agreement test for a crafted multi-value URL (all 3 facets)
Write sidebar-check-then-navigate reflects-in-chip test
Write categories-key serialization assertion for events/blog sidebar
Write crafted-URL-alone reproduces state test (no in-memory store proof)

tasks.title: Write back/forward (popstate) navigation coherence test
tasks.description:
Write a frontend/e2e test proving the browser back/forward buttons restore the exact filter state at each history entry, consistent with the URL-as-single-source model (no stale in-memory selection survives a popstate that contradicts the restored URL).

**Files:** `apps/web` e2e or integration test file (wherever existing navigation/View-Transitions tests for listings live).

**Tests:** AAA — (1) apply `?types=HOTEL`, then `?types=HOTEL,CABIN`; press Back → listing shows the `?types=HOTEL` state (chips, sidebar, grid all reflect one value); (2) press Forward → `?types=HOTEL,CABIN` state returns identically (both chips active, grid = union); (3) a popstate to any prior facet URL renders with no stale selection contradicting the restored URL.

**Acceptance mapping:** US-8 (all AC), M-7.

subtasks.title: Write back-navigation restores-prior-state test
Write forward-navigation restores-later-state test
Write no-stale-selection-survives-popstate assertion

tasks.title: Document per-facet operator model and URL-as-state principle
tasks.description:
Add a section to `apps/web/CLAUDE.md` documenting: (1) the per-facet multi-select config model (`paramKey`/`singularParamKey`/`operator`/`enum`/`dedicatedLandingPattern`) introduced in T-001, with accommodation `types` as the blueprint and events/blog `categories` as the replication; (2) the principle that the URL query param is the ONLY shared state between chips and the sidebar (no in-memory store); (3) a note that the sidebar-vs-backend latent bug (`?category=A,B` silently failing) is now closed; (4) the 2+-value SEO `noindex`/canonical rule and where its shared predicate lives, so a future facet (HOS-97 gastronomía/experiencias) can be added by declaring config, not new logic.

**Files:** `apps/web/CLAUDE.md`.

**Tests:** none (documentation-only task); verify the doc renders correctly and cross-links to `apps/web/src/lib/filters/facet-config.ts` and `apps/web/src/lib/seo/promoted-facet-canonical.ts`.

**Acceptance mapping:** supports maintainability goals from Architecture/Dependencies sections; no direct US (documentation deliverable from Implementation Approach Phase 7).

subtasks.title: Add per-facet config model section to apps/web/CLAUDE.md
Document URL-as-single-source principle and closed latent bug
Document 2+-value SEO predicate location and extensibility note

tasks.title: Write closeout.md and record decisions on the Linear issue
tasks.description:
File `.specs/HOS-96-multi-select-quick-filter-chips/closeout.md` summarizing what shipped (per-facet config model, events/blog `categories` stack, multi-toggle helper + chip/sidebar wiring, SEO 2+-value rule), which PRs implemented it, and test results (unit/model/service/API/frontend/SEO/regression suites green). Update the Linear `HOS-96` issue with the same summary plus the four resolved OQ decisions (OQ-1..OQ-4, already pinned in the spec) as a decision log. Per project convention, this feature is entirely internal (no third-party integration) so no `status-needs-smoke-staging`/`-prod` label is required by default; apply `status-needs-smoke-staging` only if the owner wants a live SEO/index verification pass, per the spec's own note.

**Files:** new `.specs/HOS-96-multi-select-quick-filter-chips/closeout.md`; Linear issue `HOS-96` (via the standard `/closeIssue` flow, not a hand-rolled state write).

**Tests:** none (closeout task); verify all prior tasks' quality gates are green before filing.

**Acceptance mapping:** closes the spec per Implementation Approach Phase 7.

subtasks.title: Write closeout.md summarizing shipped work + test results
Record OQ-1..OQ-4 decision log on the Linear issue
Apply status-needs-smoke-staging label only if owner wants live SEO verification
